title: Command Injection
description: A tool that pings a hostname provided by the user.

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Identify how an attacker can execute arbitrary commands.
stages.target: The user-provided 'hostname' is directly concatenated into a shell command string and executed.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: Provide a payload for 'hostname' to list the files in the current directory.

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: How would you rewrite this safely using the 'subprocess' module?

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: Explain why using an array of arguments in 'subprocess' is safer than a shell string.
stages.target: When using an array, the OS treats each element as a separate argument and does not invoke a shell to interpret special characters like ; or |.

hints: Special characters like ; or && can be used to chain commands in a shell.
Look into the 'subprocess' module in Python.
